Aim to achieve a target annual income of 20% in the S&P 500 using options, paid out monthly

Source: SPYT

The fund in question is Defiance S&P Income Target Fund (NYSEARCA:SPYT) and is yet another in a long line of option strategy funds that have been an unfortunate side-effect of the bull market. We go over the fundamentals and tell you why you should really stay away.

Solid Power: On The Right Path To Market Readiness

Tangerine Tan Capital | Buy

As I like to look at small companies that could potentially disrupt large industries, I came across Solid Power (NASDAQ:SLDP). And Solid Power is a company with huge potential but also risks, as it is not yet 100% certain that its technology will ultimately prevail.

But I like to build up positions in such small, interesting companies that are around 1% of my portfolio, because if the company doesn't succeed, it's a loss that doesn't really matter in the long run, but if the company is successful, I'm very happy to average up.

However, I will probably build up small positions in several players in the battery industry. Solid Power will also be one of them, as I find the technology interesting, as I think that Solid Power has competitive advantages and that they have a conservative balance sheet, and strong partnerships, and are on the right path to market readiness.

Brookfield: My Long-Term Anchor For My Portfolio

Jonah Crema | Buy

When I look for new companies to invest in, I start by picturing the world ten years from now; what will be in greater demand, which problems will need solving, and which companies will be essential to meeting those needs. From that framework of thought, I work backward to find companies positioned to thrive in that future.

Brookfield Corporation (NYSE:BN) (TSX:BN:CA) is one of them. The next decade will demand more real assets such as energy, critical infrastructure, and more capital to fund it all. Its expanding insurance and reinsurance operations take on long-dated liabilities such as annuities and pension risk transfers, creating a float of permanent capital. That float is deployed into the assets Brookfield knows best, while its asset management arm raises and earns fees from outside investors, creating a flywheel of capital generation and deployment. I invest in real companies I would be comfortable owning outright. Brookfield's large-scale, disciplined capital allocation and consistent shareholder returns make it a company I am confident holding for the long term. Based on my valuation below, I expect Brookfield to be able to return at least 20% a year over the next five years, and I rate the company a buy.

UiPath: Buy Cheaper Than The Management - Opportunistic Deep-Value Play

Juxtaposed Ideas | Buy

We previously covered UiPath Inc. (NYSE:PATH) in July 2025, discussing how the SaaS company had missed the first train for Agentic AI monetization, as observed in the decelerating growth hinted by the management's FY2026 guidance and the intensifying AI SaaS competition.

Even so, we reckoned that its market leadership in the RPA market remained compelling, significantly aided by the growing ARR and the rich cash flows. Combined with the cheap valuations and the renewed growth opportunities arising from the recently released Agentic Automation in April 2025, we had reiterated our Buy rating then.

Since then, PATH has underperformed at -7.6% against the wider market at +2.5% and its automation/ AI SaaS peers to large degrees, with the further correction to the Q3'24 bottom in the $11s/ April 2025 bottom in the $9s playing out as expected in our prior article.

This is especially since the monetization of Agentic Automation released in April 2025 has yet to flow into its FQ2'26 performance metrics, as observed in the still decelerating Annualized Recurring Revenues [ARR] growth at $1.72B (+1.7% QoQ/ +10.9% YoY) compared to a year ago at $1.55B (+3.3% QoQ/ +19.2% YoY).

On the other hand, we are already seeing early hints of improved cross-selling trends, as observed in PATH's stable Dollar-Based Net Retention Rate of 108% (inline QoQ/ -7 points YoY), compared to a quarter ago at 108% (-2 points QoQ/ -10 YoY) and a year ago at 115% (-3 points QoQ/ -7 YoY), along with the growing adj gross profit margins of 84% (inline QoQ/ +1 YoY/ -6 from FY2021 levels of 90%).

The same has been observed in its expanding operational scale at richer adj operating margins of 18.3% in H1'26 (+9.6 points YoY/ +22.3 from FY2021 levels of -4%), with it underscoring why the worst of the SaaS company's growth headwinds may already be here and brighter days may be ahead.

What's Going On With EPR Properties? I'm In Once Again

Cash Flow Venue | Buy

EPR Properties (NYSE:EPR) holds a special place in my heart. Not only is it one of the best-performing REITs in my portfolio (despite the recent pullback), but it's also the first business I had the pleasure of covering on Seeking Alpha.

Since the time I published this article (assigning EPR a "strong buy" rating), the business has provided over 34% total return, with 22% resulting from stock price change (nearly matching the broad S&P 500 index) and adding another 12 percentage points through EPR's highly attractive dividend payments. ...

However, something seems to have changed over the last couple of months, as EPR's stock price has fallen from over $61 per share to ~$53 per share. That's a double-digit correction.

Something had to change substantially for EPR to head south like that, right? Well, not necessarily, as EPR is well-known for being quite volatile, so let's investigate!

Enbridge: Buy This 5.6% Yield Midstream Company

Louis Gerard | Buy

Since my last analysis on Enbridge, the company has posted a strong total return of 8.01% on the back of strong quarterly earnings and a slight increase in distribution. I want to offer a fresh review on one of the most crucial energy companies on the market.

As a reminder, Enbridge (NYSE:ENB) (TSE:ENB) is a major Canadian midstream company that operates the longest and most complex crude and liquids transportation system, as in the Mainline, highlighting the prowess of the company. Recently, the company has been focusing on modernizing its operations as well as expanding into renewables. With the recent earnings and the recent stock appreciation, I believe that now is the right time to offer a refreshed view on the Canadian giant.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.
